title: Add distraction-free Focus Mode for deep drafting sessions
description:
## Problem
Writers lose momentum when the editor UI competes for attention, especially during long drafting sessions. The current product has strong planning surfaces but lacks an explicit distraction-free drafting mode similar to the best writer-first tools.

## Target user
Daily fiction and long-form writers who do focused drafting sprints and want a clean, low-noise writing environment.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase average words written per active writing session
- Increase percentage of sessions longer than 20 minutes
- Increase weekly active writers who complete at least 3 focused sessions

## Scope
- Add a toggleable Focus Mode in the editor that minimizes non-essential UI (left/right side panels, secondary controls, visual noise).
- Keep only core drafting controls visible: title, body editor, exit focus mode, and minimal save/status indicator.
- Add typewriter-scroll option (cursor line stays in upper-middle viewport) with persisted preference.
- Add optional session timer presets (15/30/45 minutes) with lightweight end-of-session summary (time and words written).
- Persist focus preferences per user/browser.

## Non-goals
- Gamification systems (badges, streak rewards, social leaderboards)
- AI writing assistance changes in this ticket
- Redesigning the full editor outside Focus Mode

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can enter and exit Focus Mode from the story editor with one action.
- In Focus Mode, non-essential panels are hidden and the editor remains fully usable with keyboard shortcuts.
- Typewriter-scroll can be toggled on/off and persists across refresh.
- Session timer can start from preset durations and shows elapsed/remaining time without blocking writing.
- Exiting a completed timer session shows a summary with words written in that session.

## Priority rationale
This is a core differentiator for writer trust and daily habit formation. Living Writer’s clarity is a key inspiration; shipping a superior focused drafting experience now can improve retention before expanding more advanced tooling.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship scene version history with diff preview and safe restore
description:
## Problem
Writers need confidence that drafts are never lost and that risky revisions can be undone later. Current flows emphasize drafting speed, but there is no explicit manuscript history experience for restoring earlier versions.

## Target user
Writers iterating through multiple revision passes who want to experiment safely without copying text into external backups.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce incidents of users reporting lost or overwritten content
- Increase usage of revision workflows (edits per session, return edits after pause)
- Increase retention among users with multi-session projects

## Scope
- Add automatic scene snapshots at meaningful checkpoints (manual save, significant text delta, and pre-revision-pass actions).
- Add a Version History panel for each scene with timestamped entries and optional short labels.
- Provide side-by-side diff preview (current vs selected snapshot) before restore.
- Allow one-click restore of a snapshot into the active scene with confirmation.
- Keep a short audit trail of restore actions in history.

## Non-goals
- Full Git-like branching/merge model
- Cross-story compare views
- Collaborative multi-author conflict resolution in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Scene history list shows timestamped snapshots in reverse chronological order.
- Writer can open any snapshot and preview text differences before restoring.
- Restore action requires explicit confirmation and creates a new latest snapshot (so restores are reversible).
- Snapshot creation and restore do not noticeably block editor typing performance.
- History remains available after refresh/reopen of the same story.

## Priority rationale
Trust is foundational for long-form writing products. A robust history flow reduces fear of revision and strengthens differentiation versus lightweight editors that do not protect iterative drafting deeply.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Add manuscript-wide find and replace with scoped filters and preview
description:
## Problem
As manuscripts grow, writers struggle to make consistent edits across scenes (names, terms, style fixes). Current navigation support helps jumping, but there is no dedicated manuscript-wide find/replace workflow with context previews.

## Target user
Writers editing medium-to-long manuscripts who need fast global cleanup without manually opening every scene.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce time to complete global revision tasks (character renames, terminology updates)
- Increase completion rate of revision sessions
- Increase weekly active editing sessions on manuscripts with 10+ scenes

## Scope
- Add manuscript-wide Find panel that searches title/body across all scenes in a story.
- Show grouped results by chapter/scene with snippet previews and match counts.
- Support filters (current scene, current chapter, whole manuscript) and case/whole-word toggles.
- Add Replace and Replace All with preview/confirmation, including per-result skip controls.
- Keep keyboard-first workflow (`Cmd/Ctrl+F` in scene, `Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+F` for manuscript-wide search).

## Non-goals
- Regex/advanced query syntax in v1
- Cross-story/global library search
- AI rewrite suggestions in the search flow

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can run manuscript-wide search and view contextual matches across scenes.
- Clicking a result jumps to the exact scene and highlighted occurrence.
- Replace supports single replacement and bulk replacement with confirmation.
- Replace actions provide undo for the current editing session.
- Search/replace remains performant on manuscripts with at least 100 scenes.

## Priority rationale
Global editing speed is a high-frequency pain point for serious writers and an area where polished tools stand out. Shipping this now complements existing structure/navigation work and improves real revision throughput.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
